seo_description: Current time in Vilnius, Lithuania. Eastern European Time — EET (UTC+2) in winter, EEST (UTC+3) during DST. Live clock and time differences for Vilnius.

faq.question: What time is it in Vilnius right now?
faq.answer: Vilnius, Lithuania uses Eastern European Time (EET, UTC+2) in winter and Eastern European Summer Time (EEST, UTC+3) during Daylight Saving Time. The IANA identifier is Europe/Vilnius. Lithuania follows the EU DST schedule, with clocks springing forward on the last Sunday in March and falling back on the last Sunday in October. The live clock above shows the current Vilnius time.

faq.question: What time zone is Vilnius in?
faq.answer: Vilnius, Lithuania is in the Eastern European Time Zone. It uses Eastern European Time (EET, UTC+2) in winter and Eastern European Summer Time (EEST, UTC+3) during Daylight Saving Time. The IANA identifier is Europe/Vilnius. Lithuania is an EU member state and follows EU DST rules. Vilnius shares its time zone with Riga (Latvia), Tallinn (Estonia), Helsinki (Finland), and Kyiv (Ukraine).

faq.question: Does Vilnius observe daylight saving time?
faq.answer: Yes. Vilnius, Lithuania observes Daylight Saving Time following the EU schedule. Clocks spring forward on the last Sunday in March at 3:00 AM and fall back on the last Sunday in October. Lithuania has supported ending seasonal clock changes, but EU-wide implementation has been postponed.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Vilnius and London?
faq.answer: Vilnius (EET, UTC+2) is 2 hours ahead of London (GMT, UTC+0) in winter. During UK summer (BST, UTC+1) and Lithuanian summer (EEST, UTC+3), Vilnius is still 2 hours ahead. Both follow the same clock-change schedule.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Vilnius and Warsaw?
faq.answer: Vilnius (EET/EEST, UTC+2/+3) is 1 hour ahead of Warsaw (CET/CEST, UTC+1/+2). Despite being geographically close, Lithuania is in the Eastern European Time Zone while Poland is in Central European Time. Both observe DST on the same schedule, keeping the 1-hour gap constant year-round.

faq.question: What are typical business hours in Vilnius?
faq.answer: Standard business hours in Vilnius are 9:00 AM to 6:00 PM EET/EEST, Monday through Friday. Vilnius is Lithuania's capital and home to a growing fintech sector — Lithuania has issued more fintech licenses than any other EU country. Major employers include banks, IT companies, and manufacturing. The Vilnius Stock Exchange is part of the Nasdaq Baltic market. Vilnius Old Town, one of the largest surviving medieval old towns in Europe, is a UNESCO World Heritage Site.
